title: Preterite vs imperfect — the big distinction
objectives: Choose between preterite and imperfect correctly
Map the distinction to Russian perfective vs imperfective aspect

vocab.es: siempre / a menudo / todos los días
vocab.ru: указатели на привычку (всегда, часто, каждый день)

grammar_notes: PRETERITE = a thing happened ONCE, completed, with a beginning and end. 'Ayer comí pizza.'
IMPERFECT = a thing was happening, was ongoing, was habitual. 'De niña, comía pizza todos los viernes.'
Russian map: preterite ≈ perfective (съел, написал), imperfect ≈ imperfective (ел, писал). Strong intuition for you.
Combined in narration: imperfect sets the scene, preterite advances the action. 'Llovía cuando salí.' (It was raining when I left.)
example_phrases: De niño, jugaba en el parque. (As a child, I used to play in the park.)
Ayer jugué al fútbol. (Yesterday I played football — one game.)
